analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.note: total_profiled_wall_time_ms is over-counted: layer::unique::prologue (NVTX outer range, 354 µs, 39 kernels) overlaps with individual operator NVTX ranges. True per-iteration latency is approximately 363 µs (prologue + addmm). All per-operator durations below are correct; use them for relative impact estimates rather than the aggregate.

bottleneck.description: 30 convertTensor_kernel launches (111.2 µs total) perform NCHW<->NHWC format coercions before and after every cuDNN convolution call. cuDNN prefers NHWC internally; when tensors arrive in NCHW (PyTorch default), it inserts format-conversion kernels on each convolution. Additionally, cuDNN is selecting Ampere-generation sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm_indexed_wo_smem_tf32f32 kernels on this Blackwell GPU instead of Blackwell-native WGMMA kernels — switching to channels_last enables cuDNN to select NHWC-optimized Blackwell paths. Achieved occupancy for the compute-bound convolutions is 14–15%, and NHWC tiling can sometimes improve this.

transformation.description: Set channels_last (NHWC) memory format on both the model and the input tensor in get_model_and_input(). cuDNN receives NHWC-contiguous tensors directly, skipping all convertTensor_kernel launches. cuDNN also selects NHWC-optimized implicit GEMM kernels that may use Blackwell-native WGMMA instructions.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates 15 NCHW<->NHWC round-trip format conversions per forward pass

optimizations.notes: Apply channels_last before BF16 cast so cuDNN benchmarks the correct layout with the correct dtype. All Conv2d layers in ConvBlock use kernel_size=3 with padding, which is fully compatible with channels_last. MaxPool2d and AdaptiveAvgPool2d also support channels_last input without issue.

bottleneck.description: Compute-bound convolutions (64->128, 128->256) run 65-73% tensor core active in TF32 via Ampere sm80_xmma kernels on a Blackwell GPU (RTX PRO 6000). Blackwell WGMMA BF16 delivers 1,457 TFLOPS vs ~91 TFLOPS FP32 SIMT (16x ratio per hardware table); even accounting for memory overhead, BF16 kernel selection should yield 3-4x speedup on the TC-bound portions. The linear head runs gemmSN_TN_kernel (FP32 SIMT, TC%=0) for a tiny 16x256 x 256x10 GEMM — BF16 forces cuBLAS to route through the BF16 GEMM path. BatchNorm kernels at DRAM%=67.2% will halve their memory traffic under BF16.

transformation.description: Cast model weights and input tensor to BF16 in get_model_and_input(). This forces cuDNN to select Blackwell-native BF16 WGMMA kernels for all Conv2d stages (replacing Ampere sm80_xmma), routes gemmSN_TN_kernel to a BF16 GEMM path with tensor core utilization, and halves the DRAM traffic for all BatchNorm element-wise Triton kernels.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~2x for BatchNorm passes; ~3-4x arithmetic throughput for compute-bound convolutions on Blackwell WGMMA vs Ampere HMMA TF32

optimizations.notes: BF16 accumulates rounding errors but is well-established for CNN inference. The workload is eval()-mode classification — BF16 is standard practice. Verify numerical equivalence by comparing torch.allclose(fp32_out, bf16_out.float(), atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2). Depends on OPT-1 (channels_last) being applied first so cuDNN benchmarks Blackwell NHWC BF16 kernels.

bottleneck.description: BatchNorm at eval() time is a pure affine transform: y = (x - running_mean) / sqrt(running_var + eps) * gamma + beta. The running_mean and running_var are frozen constants; the entire operation reduces to a per-channel scale and shift on the conv output. This can be folded algebraically into the preceding Conv2d weights and biases, eliminating all 14 kernel launches. The kernels are memory-bound at 67.2% DRAM throughput — each reads the full feature map, scales it, and writes it back, providing zero reuse.

transformation.description: FX graph pass: detect aten.cudnn_convolution.default nodes whose sole or primary consumer is aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default. Fold the BN gamma/beta/running_mean/running_var into the Conv2d weight and bias. The folded weight absorbs the channel-wise normalization scale; the folded bias absorbs the mean shift and the additive beta offset. The BN node is removed from the graph.
fx_steps: Iterate gm.graph.nodes in topological order; collect all nodes with target == torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default.

For each bn_node: verify its first input (the activation) comes directly from a node with target == torch.ops.aten.cudnn_convolution.default (no intervening ops). Skip bn_nodes where the activation has multiple users — folding would change semantics for the other users.
Extract bn_node.args: (input, weight=gamma, bias=beta, running_mean, running_var, momentum, eps). Retrieve their tensor values via node.meta or by calling getattr on gm for get_attr nodes.
Compute fold scale: scale = gamma / torch.sqrt(running_var + eps). Shape: (out_channels,).

Compute folded conv weight: w_new = conv_weight * scale.reshape(-1, 1, 1, 1) where conv_weight has shape (out_channels, in_channels, kH, kW). Handle the case where the conv has no bias (set conv_bias = torch.zeros(out_channels) if None).
Compute folded conv bias: b_new = (conv_bias - running_mean) * scale + beta.

Register w_new and b_new as new parameters on the GraphModule: gm.register_parameter('bn_fold_weight_N', nn.Parameter(w_new, requires_grad=False)) and similarly for bias. Insert get_attr nodes for each in the graph immediately before conv_node.
Update conv_node.args: replace the weight and bias get_attr references with the new bn_fold_weight/bias get_attr nodes.

bn_node returns a tuple (output, mean, rstd). Replace all uses of bn_node's tuple output index 0 (the normalized activation) with conv_node directly. If bn_node.users contains getitem(bn_node, 0) nodes, replace those getitem nodes' uses with conv_node.
graph.erase_node(bn_node) for each bn_node processed (after all users are redirected). Also erase any now-dead getitem nodes.
transformation.code_hint:
# scale = gamma / sqrt(var + eps)
# w_new = conv_w * scale[:, None, None, None]
# b_new = (conv_b - mean) * scale + beta
# Replace conv weight/bias, erase bn node

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates 14 element-wise read-scale-shift-write passes over full feature maps

optimizations.notes: Only correct at eval() time — running_mean and running_var must be frozen (not updated). The workload calls model.eval() so this is safe. The folded parameters must be materialized as new tensors (not computed symbolically) because Inductor needs concrete weight buffers at compile time. After applying OPT-2 (BF16), the folded parameters will already be BF16 since the model was cast before compilation — no additional dtype handling needed.

global_notes: Profile double-counting: layer::unique::prologue is an outer NVTX range that wraps the same kernels as the individual operator NVTX sub-ranges. The reported total_profiled_wall_time_ms of 1.069 ms over-counts; true per-iteration latency is approximately 363 µs. Use per-operator duration_ns for relative impact estimates.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is NULL in all operators — this counter was removed in the Blackwell architecture. Warp stall analysis uses eligible_cycles_pct instead; observed at 21–26% across operators, indicating scheduler stalls (ideal is ~100%).

cuDNN kernel selection: the current sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm_indexed_wo_smem_tf32f32 kernel is an Ampere (sm80) kernel running on Blackwell. Applying OPT-1 (channels_last) and OPT-2 (BF16) together should trigger cuDNN to select Blackwell-native NHWC BF16 WGMMA kernels, which have significantly higher throughput.

The linear head (aten::addmm, 16x256 x 256x10) is 0.8% of total time and under-utilizes the GPU due to the tiny GEMM size. It is not worth further optimizing — even optimal kernel selection cannot make this GEMM efficient at batch=16.

Apply optimizations in order: OPT-1 (channels_last in get_model_and_input) → OPT-2 (BF16 in get_model_and_input) → OPT-3 (BN fold FX pass at torch.compile time). The non-graph changes take effect before FX graph compilation; the BN fold pass runs during compilation.
Blackwell hardware limits used: Peak BF16 = 1,457 TFLOPS, Peak FP32 = 91 TFLOPS, Peak HBM BW = 1.79 TB/s, Ridge Point BF16 = 814 FLOP/byte (from hardware-limits.md).
